name: Cashew Nut Chicken (Yiu Gwoh Gai)
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Chicken or 2 whole chicken breasts
20 small Chinese mushrooms (or 1 medium-size can mushrooms)
1/2 cup Bamboo shoot tips (water packed in 15 oz-can)
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------
MARINADE-------------------------
3/4 tsp Salt
1/2 tsp Sugar
1 tsp Thin soy sauce
1 tbsp Oyster sauce
Dash of pepper
1 tbsp Cornstarch
2 Green onions, slivered
3 Thin slices ginger root, slivered
1. Skin and bone chicken. Cut into 2-inch x 1/2-inch pieces.
2. Sprinkle the chicken with each of the ingredients listed under "marinade," mix well, then add the green onions and ginger. Marinate for 1 hour.
3. Heat 2 cups oil to 325 degrees Fahrenheit and deep-fry cashew nuts for 5 minutes. Drain and salt lightly.
4. Boil Chinese mushrooms for 10 minutes. Rinse and squeeze dry. Cut off and discard stems. If using canned mushrooms, omit this step.
5. Cut celery into 1 1/2-inch pieces. Then cut each piece lengthwise into strips (julienne style).
6. Cut bamboo shoot tips into thin slices.
7. Peel water chestnuts (using a potato peeler) and cut off the top and bottom of each one. Then cut them into thin slices and soak in water to prevent them from turning dark. (Canned water chestnuts are already peeled.)
8. Heat wok, add 1 tablespoon oil, and stir-fry bamboo tips and water chestnuts together (if using fresh). Set aside.
9. Heat wok, add 1 tablespoon oil, and stir-fry mushrooms, celery, and yellow onion for 3 minutes with 1/2 teaspoon salt and 1/2 teaspoon sugar. Set aside.
10. Heat wok, add 2 tablespoons oil, and stir-fry marinated chicken for 3 minutes. Add chicken stock and cover. Cook over medium heat for 10 minutes.
11. Add the vegetables that have been previously set aside and bring to a fast boil.
12. Thicken with a mixture made from cornstarch and 2 tablespoons cold water. Bring to a boil, cook for 1 minute, then turn off the heat.
13. Add cashew nuts, mix thoroughly, and serve alone or with rice.
SOURCE: Chopsticks, Clever and Wok.
